title: 以...为主 (yǐ...wéi zhǔ) - Mainly/Primarily
explanation: 以...为主 means "to take something as the main focus" or "primarily."

examples.chinese: 这个地区以农业为主。
examples.pinyin: Zhège dìqū yǐ nóngyè wéi zhǔ.
examples.english: This area is primarily focused on agriculture.

examples.chinese: 我们的饮食以米饭为主。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen de yǐnshí yǐ mǐfàn wéi zhǔ.
examples.english: Our diet is mainly rice-based.

title: 不得不 (bùdébù) - Have no choice but to
explanation: 不得不 expresses that someone has no choice but to do something, implying reluctance.

examples.chinese: 因为堵车，我不得不走路去。
examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi dǔchē, wǒ bùdébù zǒulù qù.
examples.english: Because of the traffic jam, I had no choice but to walk.

examples.chinese: 他不得不接受这个事实。
examples.pinyin: Tā bùdébù jiēshòu zhège shìshí.
examples.english: He had no choice but to accept this fact.

title: 难免 (nánmiǎn) - Hard to avoid/Inevitable
explanation: 难免 indicates that something is hard to avoid or inevitable.

examples.chinese: 刚开始学，难免会犯错。
examples.pinyin: Gāng kāishǐ xué, nánmiǎn huì fàn cuò.
examples.english: When you first start learning, mistakes are inevitable.

examples.chinese: 一个人在外面，难免会想家。
examples.pinyin: Yí gè rén zài wàimiàn, nánmiǎn huì xiǎng jiā.
examples.english: Being alone away from home, it's hard not to feel homesick.

title: 未必 (wèibì) - Not necessarily
explanation: 未必 means "not necessarily" and is used to express doubt about an assumption.

examples.chinese: 贵的东西未必好。
examples.pinyin: Guì de dōngxi wèibì hǎo.
examples.english: Expensive things are not necessarily good.

examples.chinese: 他说的未必是真的。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuō de wèibì shì zhēn de.
examples.english: What he said is not necessarily true.

title: 何况 (hékuàng) - Let alone/Not to mention
explanation: 何况 means "let alone" or "not to mention," used to emphasize that if the first point is true, the second is even more so.

examples.chinese: 大人都觉得难，何况小孩子。
examples.pinyin: Dàrén dōu juéde nán, hékuàng xiǎo háizi.
examples.english: Even adults find it hard, let alone children.

examples.chinese: 我连中文都不会，何况日文。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ lián Zhōngwén dōu bú huì, hékuàng Rìwén.
examples.english: I can't even speak Chinese, let alone Japanese.

title: 尽管...还是 (jǐnguǎn...háishi) - Despite...still
explanation: 尽管...还是 means "despite A, B still happens," expressing a concession.

examples.chinese: 尽管很辛苦，他还是坚持了下来。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn hěn xīnkǔ, tā háishi jiānchí le xiàlái.
examples.english: Despite the hardship, he still persevered.

examples.chinese: 尽管医生不建议，她还是去上班了。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn yīshēng bú jiànyì, tā háishi qù shàngbān le.
examples.english: Despite the doctor's advice, she still went to work.

title: 与其...不如 (yǔqí...bùrú) - Rather than...better to
explanation: 与其...不如 means "rather than A, it would be better to B."

examples.chinese: 与其抱怨，不如行动。
examples.pinyin: Yǔqí bàoyuàn, bùrú xíngdòng.
examples.english: Rather than complaining, it's better to take action.

examples.chinese: 与其在家等着，不如出去找。
examples.pinyin: Yǔqí zài jiā děng zhe, bùrú chūqù zhǎo.
examples.english: Rather than waiting at home, it's better to go out and look.

title: 宁可...也不 (nìngkě...yě bù) - Would rather...than
explanation: 宁可...也不 means "would rather A than B," expressing a strong preference.

examples.chinese: 他宁可走路，也不坐公交车。
examples.pinyin: Tā nìngkě zǒulù, yě bú zuò gōngjiāochē.
examples.english: He would rather walk than take the bus.

examples.chinese: 我宁可少吃一顿，也不浪费食物。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ nìngkě shǎo chī yí dùn, yě bù làngfèi shíwù.
examples.english: I would rather skip a meal than waste food.

title: 一旦...就 (yídàn...jiù) - Once...then
explanation: 一旦...就 means "once something happens, then..." It implies that once the condition occurs, the result follows quickly or inevitably.

examples.chinese: 一旦决定了，就不要后悔。
examples.pinyin: Yídàn juédìng le, jiù búyào hòuhuǐ.
examples.english: Once you've decided, don't regret it.

examples.chinese: 一旦发现问题，就要马上解决。
examples.pinyin: Yídàn fāxiàn wèntí, jiù yào mǎshàng jiějué.
examples.english: Once a problem is found, it must be solved immediately.

title: 凡是...都 (fánshì...dōu) - All/Every...without exception
explanation: 凡是...都 means "every" or "all without exception," emphasizing universality.

examples.chinese: 凡是去过那里的人都说好。
examples.pinyin: Fánshì qù guò nàlǐ de rén dōu shuō hǎo.
examples.english: Everyone who has been there says it's great.

examples.chinese: 凡是认识他的人都喜欢他。
examples.pinyin: Fánshì rènshi tā de rén dōu xǐhuān tā.
examples.english: Everyone who knows him likes him.
